expectations.text: Response creates Elysia routes with /api/recipes prefix
expectations.evidence: Creates 'new Elysia({ prefix: "/api/recipes" })' with .get('/') and .post('/') routes. Routes resolve to /api/recipes and /api/recipes respectively. Uses the route module pattern with prefix.

expectations.text: Response uses t.Object for Typebox query/body validation
expectations.evidence: GET route uses t.Object for query params with q, cuisine, page, limit. POST route uses t.Object for body validation with title, description, ingredients, cook_time_minutes, cuisine. All properly typed with Typebox.

expectations.text: Response validates cuisine as enum/union type
expectations.evidence: Defines CuisineEnum as 't.Union(CUISINES.map((c) => t.Literal(c)))' where CUISINES is ['italian', 'mexican', 'japanese', 'indian', 'american']. Used in both query (filter) and body (create) schemas.

expectations.text: Response includes pagination params with defaults
expectations.evidence: Query schema includes 'page: t.Optional(t.Numeric({ default: 1, minimum: 1 }))' and 'limit: t.Optional(t.Numeric({ default: 20, minimum: 1, maximum: 100 }))'. Uses t.Numeric for auto-coercion from string query params.

expectations.text: Response defines error response schemas for 400/404
expectations.evidence: POST route defines response: { 201: RecipeSchema, 400: ValidationErrorResponse }. Global onError handler handles VALIDATION (400), NOT_FOUND (404), and default (500) cases. ErrorResponse and ValidationErrorResponse schemas defined with t.Object.
